title: Pyruvic acid or a salt thereof for use in treating vitiligo
abstract: The present invention generally relates to the treatment of vitiligo with a composition comprising pyruvic acid and/or a pyruvate salt. The composition may be formulated for application to the skin of a subject, for instance, as a gel, lotion, cream, ointment, soap, or stick. In some cases, the composition may comprise a lecithin, such as phosphatidylcholine. In certain embodiments, the lecithin is present as a liquid crystal, and/or in liposomes, micelles, or other vesicles. In some embodiments, the composition is a cream for transdermal delivery, comprising a polyenylphosphatidiylcholine.
claims: Claims CLAIMS  What is claimed is: 1. A method, comprising: applying, to the skin of a subject having vitiligo, a topical composition comprising pyruvic acid and/or a pyruvate salt. 2. The method of claim 1, wherein the composition comprises pyruvic acid. 3. The method of any one of claims 1 or 2, wherein the composition comprises a pyruvate salt. 4. The method of claim 3, wherein the pyruvate salt comprises sodium pyruvate. 5. The method of any one of claims 3 or 4, wherein the pyruvate salt comprises lithium pyruvate. 6. The method of any one of claims 3-5, wherein the pyruvate salt comprises magnesium pyruvate. 7. The method of any one of claims 1-6, wherein the pyruvic acid and/or a pyruvate salt is present at less than about 0.2% by weight. 8. The method of any one of claims 1-7, wherein the composition comprises a gel, lotion, cream, ointment, soap, or stick. 9. The method of any one of claims 1-8, wherein the formulation is substantially transparent. 10. The method of claim 10, wherein the formulation comprises an emulsion of the first phase and a second phase. 12. The method of any one of claims 10 or 11, wherein the first phase forms discrete vesicles contained within the second phase. 13. The method of any one of claims 10-12, wherein the first phase forms liposomes within the second phase. 14. The method of any one of claims 10-13, wherein the first phase forms multilamellar liposomes within the second phase. 15. The method of any one of claims 10 or 11, wherein the formulation comprises a liquid crystal structure of the first phase and a second phase. 16. The method of any one of claims 17-19, wherein the lecithin is present at at least about 10% by weight of the formulation. The method of any one of claims 17-20, wherein the lecithin is present at at least about 30% by weight of the formulation. The method of any one of claims 17-21, wherein the lecithin is present at at least about 60% by weight of the formulation. The method of any one of claims 17-22, wherein the lecithin comprises phosphatidylcholine. The method of any one of claims 23-25, wherein at least some of the phosphatidylcholine comprises a phosphatidylcholine comprising an oleic diglyceride linked to a choline ester of a phosphoric acid. The method of any one of claims 17-26, wherein the lecithin comprises polyenylphosphatidylcholine. The method of any one of claims 27 or 28, wherein at least about 30 wt% of the phosphatidylcholine is a polyenylphosphatidylcholine. The method of any one of claims 27-29, wherein the polyenylphosphatidylcholine comprises linoleic acid. The method of any one of claims 27-30, wherein the polyenylphosphatidylcholine comprises dilinoleoylphosphatidylcholine. The method of any one of claims 1-31, wherein the formulation further comprises an emulsifier. The method of any one of claims 1-32, wherein the formulation further comprises a fatty acid ester. The method of any one of claims 1-38, wherein the composition has a viscosity of at least about 20,000 cP. The method of any one of claims 1-39, wherein the subject is human. 41.
